# UC-22.15.33 ¬∑ East-West OT Traffic Crossing Intended Purdue Levels

## Description

Detects flows between OT zones that should communicate only through defined conduits, evidencing segmentation policy.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes fw and sourcetypes pan:traffic. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain zone pair matrix from ISA-62443 drawings; (2) log intra-zone noise baseline; (3) alert new apps; (4) feed exceptions with expiry. Indexes required: `index=fw`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:traffic`; Lookups: `ot_zone_pairs`, `ot_zone_pairs.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Palo Alto (2757), NetFlow.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:traffic` with `src_zone`, `dest_zone`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Maintain zone pair matrix from ISA-62443 drawings; (2) log intra-zone noise baseline; (3) alert new apps; (4) feed exceptions with expiry.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h
| lookup ot_zone_pairs.csv src_zone dest_zone OUTPUT allowed_conduit
| where isnull(allowed_conduit) OR allowed_conduit="false"
| stats sum(bytes) as bytes by src_zone, dest_zone, app
| sort - bytes
```

Understanding this SPL

**East-West OT Traffic Crossing Intended Purdue Levels (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 5.1)** ‚Äî Detects flows between OT zones that should communicate only through defined conduits, evidencing segmentation policy.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic` with `src_zone`, `dest_zone`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto (2757), NetFlow. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: fw; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=fw, sourcetype="pan:traffic", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where isnull(allowed_conduit) OR allowed_conduit="false"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_zone, dest_zone, app** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**East-West OT Traffic Crossing Intended Purdue Levels (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 5.1)** ‚Äî Detects flows between OT zones that should communicate only through defined conduits, evidencing segmentation policy.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic` with `src_zone`, `dest_zone`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto (2757), NetFlow. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Sankey (zone flows), Table, Single value (new pairs/week).

## SPL

```spl
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h
| lookup ot_zone_pairs.csv src_zone dest_zone OUTPUT allowed_conduit
| where isnull(allowed_conduit) OR allowed_conduit="false"
| stats sum(bytes) as bytes by src_zone, dest_zone, app
| sort - bytes
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Sankey (zone flows), Table, Single value (new pairs/week). Key fields: `app`, `bytes`, `dest_zone`, `src_zone`.
